headline_original: Libya, Iraq discuss education cooperation MoU
article_snippet: Libyan Education Minister Mohamed Al-Qriyo and Iraqi Chargé d’Affaires Ahmed Al-Sahhaf discussed drafting a memorandum of understanding to strengthen cooperation in training and curriculum development. During a meeting at the ministry on Monday, the two sides reviewed cooperation in educational staff development, as well as cultural and student exchanges. Al-Qriyo also presented the ministry’s vision for advancing interactive and digital education.
qa_notes: Reliable date inside target window and Libya-relevant

headline_original: Saddam Haftar, Greek PM review military and economic cooperation
article_snippet: Deputy Commander-in-Chief of Haftar’s forces Saddam Haftar met with Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis in Athens on Monday to discuss ways to strengthen military and economic ties between the two countries. According to a statement from the media office of Haftar’s forces, the talks covered cooperation in the security, military, and economic sectors, as well as several regional and international issues of mutual concern.
qa_notes: Reliable date inside target window and Libya-relevant

headline_original: French Ambassador, Khaled Haftar hold talks on military cooperation in Benghazi
article_snippet: French Ambassador to Libya Thierry Vallat and Khaled Haftar, Chief of Staff of Haftar’s forces, held what the French Embassy described as “productive and candid” talks in Benghazi, focusing on prospects for expanding military cooperation between the two countries. According to a statement posted by the embassy, France reaffirmed its support for efforts to unify Libya’s armed forces and reiterated its call for the withdrawal of foreign fighters and forces from the country as part of broader efforts to promote stability.
qa_notes: Reliable date inside target window and Libya-relevant

headline_original: Koury, Russian Ambassador discuss structured dialogue outcomes and election efforts
article_snippet: Russian Ambassador to Libya Aydar Aganin and Deputy Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Libya Stephanie Koury held talks on the UN-led political process, focusing on the outcomes of the structured dialogue and prospects for implementing Libya’s political roadmap. The meeting, held Sunday at the UN mission headquarters in Benghazi, also reviewed the latest political, economic, and security developments, according to statements from both the Russian Embassy and the UN Support Mission in Libya.
qa_notes: Reliable date inside target window and Libya-relevant

headline_original: Protesters shut down IOM office in Tripoli over migration policies
article_snippet: Protesters on Tuesday shut down the official office of the International Organization for Migration (IOM) in Tripoli, in protest against the way illegal migration is being managed and what they describe as efforts to “resettle migrants” in Libya.

The event will bring together training officials, experts and specialists from member states to discuss capacity building in the energy sector.

headline_original: Libya Investment Authority announces assets increase to $51.8 billion
article_snippet: The Libya Investment Authority (LIA) announced that its financial assets, including cash holdings, increased to $51.8 billion during the first quarter of this year, compared to $50.9 billion at the end of last year, marking a 1.7% increase. The growth was driven by a roughly $600 million rise in the market value of its equity portfolio, as well as $307.7 million in realized returns from dividends and interest on time deposits.
qa_notes: Reliable date inside target window and Libya-relevant
